We all have a role in keeping people safe.

Safeguarding means protecting children, young people and vulnerable adults from harm, abuse and neglect. It's fundamental to high-quality health and social care. Learn how to recognise concerns, take action and find support.

Learn More

What is Safeguarding?

Safeguarding is everyone's responsibility. It means protecting children, young people and vulnerable adults from abuse, neglect and harm, while helping them live safely and reach their full potential.

Everyone has a role to play in recognising concerns, responding appropriately and helping create safer communities.

Learn More

Common Signs of Abuse & Neglect

Recognising the warning signs early can help protect someone from harm.

Emotional

  • • Low self-esteem
  • • Anxiety or fear
  • • Withdrawal
  • • Sudden mood changes

Physical

  • • Unexplained injuries
  • • Frequent bruising
  • • Burns or cuts
  • • Poor hygiene

Behavioural

  • • Aggression
  • • Avoiding certain people
  • • Changes in behaviour
  • • Poor attendance

Neglect

  • • Lack of food
  • • Poor clothing
  • • Untreated illness
  • • Unsafe living conditions

What Should You Do?

If you notice warning signs, remain calm and follow these simple steps:

1 Stay calm and listen carefully.
2 Do not promise confidentiality.
3 Record what you observed accurately.
4 Contact the appropriate safeguarding team or emergency services.
Emergency
If someone is in immediate danger, call 999 immediately.
